zones.label: Resistor
zones.summary: Limits the current flowing in a circuit
facts: Limits current in a circuit
Unit: Ohms (Ω)
Common values: 220 Ω, 1 kΩ, 10 kΩ
Tip: Use one in series with every LED to prevent burnout
Symbol: rectangle with two lines

zones.label: LED (Light-Emitting Diode)
zones.summary: Emits light when current flows from anode to cathode
facts: Emits light when current flows (anode → cathode)
Forward voltage: ~2.0 V (red) – 3.3 V (blue/white)
Typical current: 20 mA maximum
Tip: Always pair with a 220 Ω–470 Ω resistor from a 3.3 V GPIO pin
Symbol: triangle with a bar and two light-ray arrows

zones.label: Capacitor
facts: Stores and releases electrical charge
Unit: Farads (F); common: 100 µF electrolytic, 0.1 µF ceramic
Uses: Smooth power supply; filter noise; bypass capacitor
Tip: Electrolytic caps have polarity — match + and – markings
Symbol: two parallel lines (one curved for electrolytic)

zones.label: Push Button
zones.summary: A momentary switch — connected only while pressed
facts: Momentary switch — connected only while pressed
Configuration: Connect one side to GPIO, other to GND; enable internal pull-up
Debounce: Add 10 µF cap or software delay to avoid false triggers
Tip: Use Pin.PULL_UP in MicroPython for reliable readings
Symbol: two lines with a diagonal bridging bar (normally open)

zones.label: Transistor (NPN BJT, e.g., 2N2222)
zones.summary: An electronic switch — small base current controls a large collector current
facts: Acts as an electronic switch: small base current controls large collector current
GPIO → 1 kΩ resistor → Base; load connects Collector to V+; Emitter to GND
Tip: Use to drive motors, buzzers, or LEDs that need more than 20 mA
Three leads: Base (B), Collector (C), Emitter (E)

zones.label: Potentiometer (10 kΩ)
zones.summary: A variable resistor — turning the knob changes resistance
facts: Variable resistor — turning the knob changes resistance 0 Ω → 10 kΩ
Connect: Left pin to 3.3 V, Right pin to GND, Wiper pin to ADC input
Output voltage at wiper: 0 V to 3.3 V (reads 0–65535 in MicroPython read_u16())
Tip: Use for volume control, angle setting, brightness dimmer
Symbol: rectangle with an arrow pointing to the middle

quiz.question: Which component should you always place in series with an LED to prevent it from burning out?
quiz.explanation: A resistor limits current in a circuit. Pair a 220 Ω–470 Ω resistor in series with every LED from a 3.3 V GPIO pin so the LED never draws more than its 20 mA maximum.

quiz.question: Which component acts as an electronic switch where a small base current controls a larger collector current?
quiz.explanation: An NPN BJT transistor (e.g., 2N2222) lets a small base current control a large collector current. Wire GPIO → 1 kΩ resistor → Base to drive motors, buzzers, or high-current LEDs.

quiz.question: Which component is a variable resistor you connect to an ADC pin to read a value from 0 to 65535?
quiz.explanation: A 10 kΩ potentiometer is a variable resistor. Wire its left pin to 3.3 V, right pin to GND, and the wiper to an ADC input — read_u16() returns 0–65535 as you turn the knob.

quiz.question: Which component stores and releases electrical charge and is measured in Farads?
quiz.explanation: A capacitor stores and releases electrical charge and is measured in Farads (F). Common values are 100 µF electrolytic and 0.1 µF ceramic, used to smooth power and filter noise.

quiz.question: Which component is a momentary switch that connects only while it is pressed?
quiz.explanation: A push button is a momentary switch — it conducts only while held down. Connect one side to a GPIO pin and the other to GND, then enable Pin.PULL_UP in MicroPython for reliable readings.
